title: EUV Lithography Yield Excursion Root-Cause and Lot Disposition Optimizer
expected_economic_value: $190k-$330k advanced semiconductor yield engineering / process integration role
normalized_title: EUV Lithography Yield Excursion Root-Cause and Lot Disposition Optimizer
instruction: Context: a high-volume fab sees a sudden bridge-defect and parametric-yield excursion on a high-ASP accelerator chiplet layer, and the yield engineering team must decide which lots to hold, scrap, rework, or release before downstream packaging consumes scarce interposer capacity. Task packet: product=ACCEL-X3 chiplet_layer=M2_cut EUV; die_per_wafer=612; good_die_value_usd=148; downstream_packaging_slot_value_usd=42000 per lot; baseline_yield_pct=91.8 baseline_bridge_defects_per_cm2=0.018 baseline_cd_mean_nm=18.0 baseline_cd_sigma_nm=0.62; control_limits cd_mean_nm 17.4-18.6, cd_sigma_nm<=0.82, overlay_3sigma_nm<=2.7, bridge_defects_per_cm2<=0.035, stochastic_missing_via_ppm<=7.5; lots: L41 scanner=S3 resist=R7 dose_mJ=31.2 focus_nm=-8 post_exposure_bake_C=94.5 overlay_3sigma=2.1 cd_mean=18.1 cd_sigma=0.66 bridge_defects=0.021 missing_via_ppm=5.8 e_test_yield_pct=91.2 wafers=24, L42 S3 R7 dose=30.8 focus=-11 bake=94.6 overlay=2.4 cd_mean=18.0 sigma=0.71 bridge=0.028 missing=6.3 yield=89.9 wafers=24, L43 S5 R7 dose=29.6 focus=-18 bake=95.8 overlay=3.4 cd_mean=17.2 sigma=0.96 bridge=0.071 missing=15.4 yield=78.6 wafers=25, L44 S5 R7 dose=29.4 focus=-21 bake=96.1 overlay=3.8 cd_mean=17.0 sigma=1.04 bridge=0.084 missing=19.6 yield=72.8 wafers=25, L45 S2 R9 dose=31.0 focus=-7 bake=94.3 overlay=2.2 cd_mean=18.2 sigma=0.64 bridge=0.020 missing=5.5 yield=91.5 wafers=24, L46 S5 R9 dose=30.1 focus=-19 bake=95.9 overlay=3.1 cd_mean=17.4 sigma=0.89 bridge=0.052 missing=10.8 yield=82.4 wafers=25; actions: A1 scanner_S5_focus_recenter cost_usd=180000 expected focus_plus_nm=14 overlay_minus_nm=0.8 applies_future_only, A2 hold_and_electrical_rescreen cost=52000 per_lot recovers 35% of yield_loss_vs_baseline if bridge<=0.06, A3 scrap_lot avoids packaging_slot_loss but loses wafer_value, A4 release_with_sort_guard cost=18000 per_lot allowed only if all hard control limits pass; max_excursion_budget_usd=310000. Rules and constraints: hard_fail if any control limit breached; root_cause_score for scanner = count of hard_fail lots by scanner plus mean absolute focus deviation/10 plus mean overlay_excess; financial_loss_lot=(baseline_yield-yield)/100*die_per_wafer*wafers*good_die_value plus packaging_slot_value if a hard-fail lot is incorrectly released; recommend lot disposition and one future corrective action set under budget minimizing expected loss. Required work: deliver euv_yield_excursion_analysis.xlsx + fab_disposition_memo.md with SPC flags, root-cause ranking, lot economics, action optimization, and a clear hold/scrap/rescreen/release decision. Use only supplied data; no fab tool software is required. This task is self-contained: use the task packet as the factual record, do not add source references or external datasets, and preserve a high-fidelity audit trail for every calculation, classification, and recommendation. State assumptions and unresolved evidence gaps explicitly. When executed locally, place final deliverables under the artifacts/ directory.
allowed_tools: Local text editor or Markdown editor
Local spreadsheet, CSV, JSON, YAML, or scripting tools for deterministic calculations
Local plotting or diagramming tools when useful
disallowed_tools: External fact lookup or live data pulls
Live production systems, cloud accounts, trading systems, clinical systems, or industrial controls
Secret discovery, credential testing, or any action outside the prepared task workspace
input_requirements: Use the task packet as the sole factual input.
Cite row IDs, record IDs, thresholds, formulas, and option IDs from the packet wherever they control a conclusion.
Mark missing, contradictory, or under-supported values as needs_review instead of inventing them.
inputs: All factual inputs are embedded in the task packet.
Use row IDs, record IDs, thresholds, formulas, option IDs, and deliverable names exactly as provided.
output_requirements: Produce the required deliverable files with the specified filenames when filenames are provided.
Include intermediate calculations or executable logic sufficient for another reviewer to reproduce the result.
Include validation checks, sensitivity notes around controlling thresholds, and a final decision or recommendation table.
constraints: Use the task packet as the factual record.
Do not browse, call live services, import external datasets, or invent missing values.
Treat regulated or safety-critical outputs as review packets, not authorization to act.
quality_bar: Treat the task as realistic professional work with domain-appropriate rigor.
Preserve the packet's domain detail, identifiers, thresholds, and edge cases in the final deliverables.
Make every recommendation traceable to explicit task-packet evidence or mark it as needs_review.
validation_expectations: Check required fields, row counts, totals, thresholds, and classifications before finalizing.
Show enough formulas, script logic, or tables for an independent reviewer to reproduce the result.
Flag any contradiction, missing input, or threshold-sensitive conclusion.
edge_cases: If packet evidence is incomplete, do not infer missing values; label the item needs_review.
If multiple options satisfy hard constraints, rank them using the packet's stated priorities and explain ties.
If a regulated or safety-critical conclusion appears, frame the output as a review packet rather than authorization to act.
privacy_safety_expectations: Do not use live systems, private accounts, credentials, or external datasets.
Do not include raw secrets or operational instructions that would execute real-world changes.
Keep synthetic identifiers from the packet only when needed for verification.
